steps.title: Use a two-point regression shortcut
steps.content: The expression is linear, so two x-values are enough to identify the equivalent line. Enter the expression at $x=0$ and $x=6$ in Desmos.

steps.title: Fit the line
steps.content:
Run the linear regression:
$$y_1\sim mx_1+b$$
Desmos returns $m=-\frac{1}{6}$ and $b=8$, so the equivalent expression is $-\frac{1}{6}x+8$.

steps.title: Confirm with algebra
steps.content:
Distribute the minus sign:
$$\frac{1}{2}x+3-\frac{2}{3}x+5$$
Then combine like terms:
$$\left(\frac{1}{2}-\frac{2}{3}\right)x+8=-\frac{1}{6}x+8$$
That matches choice A.
